figures.caption: FIG. 5. The distribution ρ of the values of γ (1) weight (a) and γ (2) weight (b) obtained from the sampling at T best of the original and reduced transformers (solid lines). The dotted red lines indicate the distribution of γ (1/2)

FIG. 2. A scheme of the transformer model (upper panel) and of the corresponding feed–forward model. The input sequence x is mapped to embeddings by E and combined with positional encoding (icon: white circle with a wavy line). Lightviolet boxes denote trainable linear maps (matrices), while circled ”+” and ”→” indicate elementwise addition (residual merge) and matrix multiplication, respectively; dashed arrows denote skip/residual connections. Transformer block (repeated L times). In the attention sub-layer, the attention weights A are obtained by first linearly projecting the input with W (QK) and then computing dot products between these projected vectors and all input vectors (via the transposed input), producing a score for each pair of tokens. A softmax (icon: slanted-circle in the attention branch) is applied to these scores to yield normalized attention weights, which are then used to weight the value projection computed by W (V C). The resulting attention output is added to the skip path and normalized (learnable layers, e.g., LayerNorm with trainable scale and bias). The feed-forward sub-layer applies W (1) followed by a ReLU nonlinearity (icon: beige circle with horizontal/diagonal mark), injects positional encoding a second time (same wavy-circle icon), and projects through W (2) before the residual addition and a second normalization. Poolingaugmented variant. From the normalized per-site representation X ↑ Rn↑d, a masked column-mean over non-masked sites produces a global vector g ↑ Rd, which is replicated n times to form G ↑ Rn↑d (all rows identical). The model concatenates x and G along the feature dimension (Concataxis=1), yielding [x|G] ↑ Rn↑2d, and processes the result with the same two-layer feed-forward/residual/normalization structure (using a residual projection W (r) for right residual dimension). The final network output is denoted OUT.

FIG. 6. Left column, the mean attention matrix, symmetrized and averaged over the test set sequences, for the TF1 (upper row) and for the TF4 (lower row) models, obtained during a sampling a temperature T = 2T1/2 and T = 1.1T1/2 respectively. Center column, the contact map obtained from the mean attention matrix using P@L; true positives are in blue, false positives in pink and false negatives in gray. Right column, the contact map obtained from the mean attention matrix using P@L1 NT.
